question_title: What will the Ifo Business Climate Index level (points) for Germany be for March 2026?
summary: The Ifo Business Climate Index has been range-bound at 87.6-88.4 since September 2025, with the most recent reading at 87.6 for January 2026 (unchanged from December). Key factors: (1) Germany is expected to enter a recovery phase in 2026 with 1.1-1.4% GDP growth driven by fiscal expansion, which could lift sentiment; (2) However, Trump's tariff threats (10% from Feb 1, potentially 25% in June) create significant downside risk for export-dependent Germany; (3) ZEW sentiment surged to a 4-year high, suggesting financial market optimism, but this hasn't yet translated to the Ifo business survey; (4) Monthly volatility is ~0.65 points, and with 2 months to go, the index could drift meaningfully in either direction. My central estimate is around 87.5-88.0, slightly below the recent range midpoint, reflecting the tension between recovery expectations and tariff headwinds.

factors.description: Current Ifo level at 87.6 for Jan 2026, flat for 2 months — strong anchor for near-term forecast
Germany expected to grow 1.1-1.4% in 2026 with fiscal expansion (€500bn infrastructure fund, defense) — supports modest improvement
Trump tariffs (10% from Feb 1, potentially 25% in June) threaten German exports and could depress business sentiment
ZEW Economic Sentiment surged to 59.6 (4-year high) in Jan 2026 — forward-looking optimism not yet reflected in Ifo
Recent 3-month trend slightly negative (-0.4, -0.4, 0.0) — mild downward momentum
Manufacturing showed sharp improvement in Jan 2026 Ifo — could lead broader recovery

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the Ifo Business Climate Index level for March 2026 (in points) as published after the time the questionby the Ifo Institute. The primary source is the Ifo Institute’s “Ifo Business Climate” page: [https://www.ifo.de/en/ifo-business-climate](https://www.ifo.de/en/ifo-business-climate). Use the published index level corresponding to March 2026 as shown in the official release/materials linked from that page.
fine_print: Record the index level exactly as published by Ifo (including any decimal places shown). Use the headline “Business Climate Index” level (not subcomponents such as expectations/current assessment unless explicitly labeled as the index level). If Ifo revises the value before May 1, 2026, use the latest official value available on the primary source as of resolution time. If the primary source is unavailable, use Ifo’s press release archive for business climate releases. Candidate Sources include [https://www.ifo.de/en/press](https://www.ifo.de/en/press) and [https://tradingeconomics.com/germany/business-confidence](https://tradingeconomics.com/germany/business-confidence). If a dispute arises about interpretation, apply the Metaculus credible-sources policy at [https://www.metaculus.com/faq/.](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/.)
background_info:
The Ifo Business Climate Index is a closely watched forward-looking sentiment indicator for the German economy and can influence market expectations for euro area growth.
